description_long: This object is a handheld flashlight with a cylindrical black body and a slightly flared head, designed for portable illumination. The main body is smooth and features a prominent red sliding switch near the head, which serves as the primary control for turning the light on and off. The head of the flashlight is wider than the body, likely housing a bulb or LED and a reflector for focusing the beam. The tail end is flat, allowing the flashlight to stand upright, and the overall construction appears to be made of durable plastic, possibly with some internal metal components for electrical conductivity. The design is utilitarian, with no visible branding or decorative elements, emphasizing function over form. This type of flashlight is commonly used in urban environments for emergency lighting, outdoor activities, or as a tool in garages and workshops.
description: A black cylindrical handheld flashlight with a red sliding switch and a slightly flared head, designed for portable use. The body is smooth and utilitarian, with a flat tail for upright standing.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the black flashlight, with the red sliding switch clearly visible near the head.
description_view_1: Front view looking directly at the lens and head of the flashlight, with the red switch visible on top.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, displaying the cylindrical body and the red switch, mirroring view 0.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the flat tail end of the flashlight and the red switch on top.
